title: Om Namah Shivay Dhun

lines.lyrics: Om Namah Shivay Om Namah Shivay
lines.indian: Ni ReGa GaGa Re Ga, GaRe ReGaPaGa Re~Ni.
Ni Dha Re Ni Dha.Pa. Pa.Dha. Ni.Re ReGa
Dha Pa Ni Dha Re Ni Ga Re Pa Ga Dha Pa Ni
Ga Ga Ga Ga Pa Dha Ni Ni Ni NiReNiDha Ni
Dha Dha Dha DhaPa PaDha NiDha PaGa

description: The "Om Namah Shivay Dhun" is a meditative and devotional chant centered around the sacred 'Panchakshara Mantra' dedicated to Lord Shiva. As a 'dhun', it features a simple, repetitive melodic phrase designed to induce a state of tranquility and spiritual focus. Its timeless melody and profound spiritual resonance make it a cornerstone of Hindu devotional music, often used for meditation, prayer, and congregational chanting.
trivia: The mantra 'Om Namah Shivay' translates to 'I bow to Shiva' and is one of the most important mantras in Shaivism, a major tradition within Hinduism.

It is often referred to as the 'Panchakshara Mantra' (five-syllable mantra) because of its core five syllables: Na, Ma, Shi, Va, Ya, which are believed to represent the five elements (earth, water, fire, air, ether) and the five faces of Shiva.

While many versions exist, a 'dhun' typically emphasizes a continuous, flowing melody rather than a structured composition, making it ideal for sustained chanting and creating a meditative atmosphere.
The chant is widely used in yoga and meditation practices globally, transcending its religious origins to become a universal symbol of peace and spiritual connection.
